title: CS wants officials to expedite works ahead of launch of digital health cards on June 2
content: Mr. Ramakrishna Rao directs officials to speed up redressal of matters other than finance related ones

title: DMRC rolls out hydrogen buses in Central Vista
content: The service will operate on all working days from Monday to Friday, excluding gazetted holidays, during peak office hours between 8:30 a.m. and 12:30 p.m. and again from 3:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m.

title: Petrol and diesel prices hike LIVE: ‘Take votes first, then raise prices’, Congress attacks Modi
content: Amid pressure on fuel supplies, oil-marketing companies hiked petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 across all variants

title: Unemployed ‘youngsters like cockroaches’ become media, activists; attack system: CJI
content: The comments came while a Bench of CJI Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi was pulling up a lawyer for “pursuing” a senior advocate designation. It said there were already “parasites” in society who attack the system and asked the petitioner whether he wanted to join hands with them.

title: Kerala politics highlights: Satheesan meets Pinarayi Vijayan, calls on senior leaders in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala said that the CM has the prerogative to decide the composition of the Cabinet

title: Congress eyes comeback in Shillong Lok Sabha seat
content: The parliamentary constituency, one of two in Meghalaya, fell vacant after the death of the Voice of the People Party’s Ricky Andrew J. Syngkon, who had ended the Congress’s 26-year run in 2024

title: Viksit Bharat journey must be rooted in ethics, social harmony: Delhi L-G tells Hansraj College’s students
content: Delhi LG Taranjit Singh Sandhu underlined that public authority is a responsibility, not a privilege, and that governance must always remain grounded in integrity, empathy and service to the people

title: Vaiko to protest demanding urgent repairs to Shenbagavalli Dam
content: Pointing out that water stored in a dam built in 1773 AD across the Shenbagavalli river benefits farmers in both Tamil Nadu and Kerala, Mr. Vaiko called for the repair of cracks that appeared in the dam around 1935 and the reconstruction of a breached portion of the main canal

title: The rise of Satheesan: What it means for the Congress, Muslim League and Kerala politics
content: V.D. Satheesan is now the unquestionable leader of Kerala Muslims and Christians, around half of the State’s population; over the past five years, he has carefully crafted his political positions

title: India’s first satellite-tagged Ganges soft-shell turtle released in Kaziranga
content: The release of the freshwater reptile coincided with the observation of Endangered Species Day; Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma termed the event a major step for wildlife conservation

title: Three minor boys arrested for blackmailing schoolgirl, circulating private videos in Krishnagiri
content: The trio created a fake account and sent her a follow request on Instagram. They downloaded her images from her profile and had them morphed into unrelated obscene photos, which they later used to blackmail her

title: With roles reversed, Satheesan makes courtesy call on Pinarayi
content: The fierce rivals in the Assembly had a warm meeting at Pinarayi’s house as the mantle of chief ministership passes to Satheesan

title: AIADMK split: Velumani faction willing to hold talks with Palaniswami; rebel Veeramani backs EPS
content: Referring to Mr. Palaniswami’s statement that he was ready to make any sacrifice, Mr. Velumani said that the AIADMK general secretary should invite his faction for a discussion, at least once

title: Karnataka High Court directs Centre to act on actor Darshan’s complaint of ‘media trial’
content: “Courts cannot permit the course of justice to be overshadowed by the glare of studio lights”, says HC

title: Allahabad HC grants bail to group of men held over Ganga iftar row
content: According to the complaint filed by Rajat Jaiswal, the President of the Varanasi chapter of the BJP Yuva Morcha, the accused broke their Ramzan fast on a boat in the Ganga on March 15, ate meat, and threw the leftover waste into the “holy” river

title: BDA plans to develop 11 layouts along PRR, runs into same old land acquisition hurdles
content: Many farmers said they were awaiting the issuance of a preliminary notification by the BDA so they could challenge it before the Karnataka High Court.

title: AICC General Secretary calls for comprehensive policy to combat drug abuse in J&K
content: Ghulam Ahmad Mir said the issue should not be viewed merely through the prism of revenue generation, but through its long-term social, moral and health implications on families and society

title: Vellore Corporation installs free drinking water points at 20 spots for public during summer
content: Corporation officials said that water supply to free water distribution points will not affect regular water supply to households

title: Why IUML firmly backed V.D. Satheesan for Kerala Chief Minister
content: Political analysts say the former Leader of the Opposition cultivated strong acceptance among Muslims and many see him as “one among us”

title: Bihar CM hands over ₹1 crore cheque to cricketer Ishan Kishan for T-20 World Cup heroics
content: The 27-year-old wicket-keeper batsman received a warm welcome from Mr. Choudhary, who placed a stole around Kishan's neck and presented him with a memento before handing over the cheque

title: India can play ‘greater role’ for peace in West Asia, says Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi
content: Abbas Araghchi said Iran is ready to help pass all vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, adding the situation in Strait of Hormuz is very complicated now

title: Every rupee counts: Hyderabad reacts as petrol price crosses ₹110 mark
content: People in Hyderabad speak on domino effect of the fuel price hike on groceries, transport and other expenses

title: Once stronghold of maoists, Karregutalu to become accessible as road nears completion
content: The road is expected to benefit local communities, particularly Gotti Koya tribals, by improving access to transportation, healthcare, education and emergency services

title: UCC to ensure justice for every citizen: Assam CM Himanta
content: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Sarma asserted that tribal communities will be kept out of the purview of the UCC to protect their traditions and customs

title: Supreme Court allows actor Darshan to revive bail plea in a year if no ‘substantial progress’ in trial
content: The top court observed that the trial in the 2024 Renukaswamy murder case was moving at an “extremely slow” pace with only 10 witnesses examined in seven months

title: Manipur violence probe panel gets six-month extension to submit report
content: According to a notification, the Commission of Inquiry shall submit its report to the Centre ‘as soon as possible but not later than November, 20, 2026’

title: How Tagore’s songs were ahead of their time in form and feeling
content: The Nobel-laureate’s songs, popularly referred to as Rabindra Sangeet, are dynamic texts open to reinterpretation, allowing each generation to find new meaning in themes of freedom, love, and humanity — revisiting the Tagore’s musical oeuvre on his 165th birth anniversary

title: 99.07% pass in Kerala SSLC results
content: The pass percentage was 0.43 percentage points less than the 99.5% last year; 30,514 students secure full A+ grade

title: Fuel price hike: Petrol crosses ₹106 in Bengaluru, diesel at ₹94
content: CM Siddaramaiah and DCM D.K. Shivakumar criticised the price hike and question the Modi government for burdening the public

title: Luxury bus on the way to scrap yard catches fire in Vellore
content: The police removed the charred vehicle from the carriageway to resume traffic movement on the stretch

title: Public paying price for Modi government’s mistake: Rahul on fuel hike
content: The Rs 3 shock has already arrived, the rest of the 'vasooli (recovery)' will be done in instalments, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi in a post on X

title: Jayalalithaa’s picture still remains in my pocket: TVK Minister Sengottaiyan
content: On TVK’s victory, Mr. Sengottaiyan said Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has created history, effectively eliminating the practice of cash for votes.

title: Residents stage road blockade seeking removal of Tasmac outlet near Ammapettai
content: The protesters said Tasmac shop number 3802 was functioning at the Poonachi bus stop and was located about 400 metres from a government school

title: Tell your ‘petty politicians’ not to blame the court for delay, CJI tells Thackeray-Shinde factions
content: Addressing the lawyers in court when they called for an adjournment in the case related to the party name and symbol, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant said he was not one who would put up with slandering of the court by politicians in the media

title: Destroying Sanatana doesn’t mean people should not go to temples: Udhayanidhi Stalin
content: Mr. Udhayanidhi said equal rights should be given to everyone, not only in temples but in society as a whole

title: Tasmac shuts 150 outlets across Tamil Nadu in three days; salesmen face challenges from customers
content: Outlets located in close proximity to educational institutions and places of worship have been shut across Tamil Nadu following directives issued by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay

title: Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in Karnataka to begin from June 20; over 5.55 crore voters to be covered
content: V Anbu Kumar, Chief Electoral Officer (CEO) for Karnataka said that 86% of mapping has been completed so far and the remaining will be completed before the SIR exercise begins

title: Rathindra Bose, a Chartered Accountant and BJP north Bengal MLA, elected Speaker of Bengal Assembly
content: This is the first time that a leader from north Bengal has been selected for the Speaker’s post; Mr. Bose is the MLA from the Cooch Behar Dakshin Assembly seat, where he defeated Trinamool Congress’s Avijit De Bhaumik

title: Arunachal government focusses on fiscal discipline, sustainable practices after PM Modi's appeal to spend wisely
content: The State government also resolved to implement “100% e-office and paperless governance across departments by March, 2027” to streamline official functioning

title: Assam Assembly’s seniormost MLA Patowary takes oath as pro-tem Speaker
content: The first session of the Assam Assembly begins on May 21. The four-day session will continue till May 26, with the legislators to be sworn-in on the first day

title: 'Some youngsters like cockroaches': CJI on unemployed youth turning activists
content: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant likened some unemployed youth to "cockroaches" who become activists attacking institutions. He made these remarks while hearing a lawyer's plea for senior advocate designation, questioning the petitioner's conduct and social media posts. The court emphasized that senior designation is earned, not chased.

title: Blackouts & unrest: CIA chief flies to Cuba; is US-led bloodless coup coming?
content: The U.S. is reportedly employing economic pressure and political tactics to achieve a bloodless transition in Cuba, following decades of failed covert operations. CIA Director John Ratcliffe's visit to Havana signals a new approach, with Washington demanding fundamental changes for engagement, while intensifying sanctions and intelligence operations.

title: ‘To pin the blame on Hardik is unfair’: Ashwin on MI’s disappointing campaign
content: Ravichandran Ashwin defended Hardik Pandya, deeming it unfair to solely blame him for Mumbai Indians' poor IPL 2026 season. Ashwin highlighted Pandya's past success at Gujarat Titans and suggested the team as a whole failed to perform. He also expressed concern over Pandya's batting timing, hinting at potential physical issues for the 'once-in-a-generation' all-rounder.

title: Elon Musk admitted that CEO of one of world’s biggest tech company is indeed his 'true friend'
content: Elon Musk seemingly confirmed a 'true friendship' with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang by resharing a post highlighting their decade-long connection. Huang revealed he helped Musk build early Tesla computers and was his first customer for the DGX-1 supercomputer in 2016. Musk recently expressed admiration, confirming Tesla and SpaceX will continue purchasing Nvidia AI chips.

title: How nighttime raids, heliborne assaults & jungle ops made India's Special Forces legendary
content: India's elite Special Forces, across Army, Navy, and Air Force, have consistently demonstrated exceptional prowess in diverse covert and overt operations. From daring cross-border raids in Pakistan and Myanmar to critical hostage rescues in Sierra Leone and counter-terror missions in Jammu and Kashmir, these highly trained units showcase unwavering courage, precision, and strategic impact, safeguarding national interests and deterring adversaries.

title: ICC punishes Pakistan after historic Bangladesh Test defeat; fined, docked WTC points
content: Pakistan face a double blow after their shocking Test defeat to Bangladesh. The ICC has fined the team 40% of their match fee and deducted eight crucial points from their World Test Championship standings due to a slow over-rate. This significant penalty pushes Pakistan down to eighth place, impacting their championship aspirations.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: Bhojshala complex is temple of Goddess Vagdevi, Hindus have right to worship: Madhya Pradesh HC
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has reserved its decision on the religious character of the Bhojshala complex in Dhar district. Hindus claim it's a temple to Goddess Vagdevi, while Muslims assert it's the Kamal Maula mosque. Security has been heightened as the court prepares to deliver its verdict after extensive hearings.

title: IPL 2026 makes history: All 10 teams record a centurion in a single season
content: IPL 2026 has etched its name in history as every team now boasts a centurion, a first in the tournament's 19 years. From emerging talents like Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to seasoned stars like Virat Kohli and Quinton de Kock, batters across all franchises have achieved the three-figure mark, showcasing a remarkable display of batting prowess throughout the season.

title: E-commerce platform refuses to refund? Here is what you can do as per law
content: Are you finding yourself waiting too long for a refund on your online purchase? Under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 in India, you have several options at your disposal. Sellers are required to accept returns for defective products without hesitation.

title: India GCCs to gain as US plans sharp H-1B wage hikes
content: Proposed US H-1B wage hikes are poised to benefit India's Global Capability Centres (GCCs) as MNCs re-evaluate global workforce strategies. With a focus on smaller, outcome-driven teams and visa-agnostic environments, GCCs are becoming a cost-efficient alternative for companies looking to scale operations offshore amidst rising US hiring expenses.

title: Should India take over Pakistan's role to mediate US-Iran talks? What Russia said
content: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov suggested India could play a long-term diplomatic role in mediating US-Iran tensions, while Pakistan facilitates immediate dialogue. Lavrov highlighted India's diplomatic experience and global standing, noting its direct stake in West Asian stability and energy security.

title: RG Kar rape-murder case: Bengal govt suspends 3 cops for 'lapses' in probe
content: Three police officers have been suspended by the West Bengal government following alleged investigative lapses in the RG Kar rape-murder case. A departmental probe is also being initiated against former Kolkata Police Commissioner Vineet Goyal and two other officers. This action precedes the state's upcoming elections.

title: 'Of course, if I'm playing, but...': Virat Kohli breaks silence on 2027 WC aspiration
content: Virat Kohli remains committed to representing India in ODIs, emphasizing mutual respect and value as key to his continued participation. He stated he will play as long as he can contribute meaningfully and feels valued by the team, not in a phase where he needs to prove himself.

title: Fact check: Viral Chud The Builder Facebook screenshot debunked
content: Streamer Dalton “ChudTheBuilder” Eatherly was arrested after a courthouse shooting in Clarksville, Tennessee. Soon after, a viral screenshot claimed he posted racist comments on Facebook about the incident. However, there is no verified evidence the Facebook post was real. Authorities charged Eatherly with attempted murder and other firearm-related offenses following the shooting investigation.

title: Decentralise conduct of NEET-UG exam, act against NTA: IMA urges Centre after paper leak
content: The Indian Medical Association has urged the government to decentralize NEET-UG exams and implement online testing to prevent future paper leaks.  Expressing deep concern over the NEET-UG 2026 irregularities, the IMA called for a CBI probe, swift arrests, and fast-track courts to restore public trust in the selection process for aspiring doctors.

title: IPL: How Lucknow Super Giants can make or break CSK’s playoff hopes?
content: Chennai Super Kings face a critical IPL 2026 match against Lucknow Super Giants. A victory propels CSK towards the top four, boosting their playoff chances significantly. However, a loss would plunge them into a precarious situation, heavily reliant on other teams' results and potentially jeopardizing their season. This encounter is a definitive turning point for their qualification hopes.

title: Rupee hits fresh record low: Why the Indian currency breached 96 against US dollar
content: Rupee hit a fresh record low of 96.07 against the US dollar on Friday, extending its losing streak over three straight sessions amid soaring crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 95.86 against the US dollar and slipped further to 95.94, down 30 paise from its previous close.

title: IMD predicts onset of monsoon over Kerala on May 26
content: India's monsoon is expected to arrive in Kerala on May 26, eight days earlier than usual. This early onset, however, doesn't guarantee sufficient rainfall for the season, as the India Meteorological Department forecasts 'below normal' precipitation amid El Niño risks. The Met department's prediction uses a sophisticated statistical model.

title: ‘Clear case for leadership’: Shastri backs Samson as future India T20I captain
content: Former India coach Ravi Shastri champions Sanju Samson for T20I captaincy, citing his stellar performances and leadership experience. Shastri believes Samson's recent match-winning displays, particularly in the T20 World Cup and IPL, make him a strong contender to lead India within the next few years, potentially before the 2028 World Cup.

title: Watch: UAE's F-16 jets escort PM Modi's plane as he arrives in Abu Dhabi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commenced a five-nation tour in the UAE, receiving a ceremonial escort and holding high-level talks with President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. The visit aims to bolster India's partnerships in trade, technology, and clean energy with the UAE and European nations, with significant opportunities anticipated for Indian exporters.

title: How Jaipur’s Biwal family’s success story blew lid off NEET paper leak
content: NEET-UG 2026 paper leak probe has implicated the Biwal family, with multiple members securing medical college admissions. CBI arrested Dinesh, Mangilal, and Vikas Biwal, suspecting their admissions might be linked to the alleged racket. Investigators are examining if leaked papers were used, raising serious questions about the family's success.

title: Kohli reveals how RCB turned ABD and Gayle into global icons
content: Virat Kohli revealed the profound emotional connection he shares with Royal Challengers Bengaluru, highlighting how the franchise elevated players like AB de Villiers and Chris Gayle into Indian fan favorites.

title: India signs MoU on strategic petroleum, LPG supplies during PM Modi’s UAE visit
content: The two sides also signed a memorandum of understanding related to strategic petroleum reserves, along with an agreement concerning supplies of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).

title: Paper leak row: Education minister announces computer-based NEET exams from next year
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan announced NEET UG exams will be computer-based from next year, emphasizing zero tolerance for malpractices amid paper leak allegations. A re-examination is scheduled for June 21st, with the investigation handed over to the CBI to address the examination mafia and ensure fairness for deserving students.

title: Fuel gets costlier: Centre raised petrol, diesel prices by Rs 3 - key details
content: Fuel prices saw a Rs 3 per litre increase on Friday. This move follows significant losses for state-run oil companies due to soaring global crude oil prices. The conflict in West Asia disrupted key shipping routes, pushing up import costs for India. This price adjustment comes after a period of frozen rates, impacting transportation and other sectors.

title: Unnao rape case: SC quashes Delhi HC order suspending Kuldeep Singh Sengar's life term
content: The Supreme Court has overturned the Delhi High Court's suspension of former BJP MLA Kuldeep Singh Sengar's life sentence in the Unnao rape case. The apex court has ordered the High Court to re-examine the matter within two months, ensuring a fresh decision free from prior influences.

title: Noida woman, daughter-in-law of retired judge, found hanging 5 months after wedding
content: A 31-year-old woman from Noida was found hanging in her husband's Bhopal home. The woman's maternal family has accused her in-laws of murder, claiming she wanted to leave Bhopal. Police are investigating the incident, with no suicide note recovered.

title: 'Arshdeep should be banned': Ex-India star fumes over racist remark on Tilak Varma
content: Punjab Kings bowler Arshdeep Singh faces backlash after a video surfaced showing him allegedly making a racially insensitive remark to Mumbai Indians batter Tilak Varma. Former India spinner Laxman Sivaramakrishnan strongly condemned the comment, recalling his own experiences with skin tone-based mockery and demanding strict action against Arshdeep.

title: Hardik Pandya likely to return for KKR clash, confirms MI teammate
content: Mumbai Indians' captain Hardik Pandya is sidelined with a back spasm, missing three consecutive matches. All-rounder Shardul Thakur provided an update, suggesting Pandya could return for the May 20th match against Kolkata Knight Riders. Despite the team's elimination from IPL 2026, Pandya's absence and poor form have been significant factors in their disappointing season.

title: Petrol, diesel price hike: Indian Oil director calls it ‘very small rise’
content: The government on Friday raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs 3 per litre nationwide. In Delhi, petrol prices increased from Rs 94.77 to Rs 97.77 per litre, while diesel rates moved up from Rs 87.67 to Rs 90.67 per litre.

title: Price prediction: Where are gold rates headed today & what should investors do?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are seeing a weak intra-day bias, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities. He recommends a sell on rise strategy:

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: PBKS slump to 43.8% as GT and RCB tighten playoff grip
content: With playoffs in sight for some and out of reach for others, the IPL race intensifies. Gujarat Titans and Royal Challengers Bangalore are nearing qualification, while Sunrisers Hyderabad are strong contenders. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold even chances for the top four, as Punjab Kings' hopes dwindle. Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals face slim prospects.

title: Delhi gets hydrogen-fueled shuttle buses: All you need to know
content: Delhi Metro Rail Corporation has launched an integrated hydrogen-powered shuttle bus service in the Central Vista area, featuring two buses with a 35-passenger capacity each. Supported by Indian Oil Corporation Limited, this initiative aims to provide an environmentally friendly last-mile connectivity option.

title: 'When I was in the air ...': UP’s ‘flying man’ survives 50ft storm lift, lands in field
content: An e-rickshaw driver was lifted nearly 50ft and flung 100 metres by storm winds after trying to secure a tin shed. He landed in a sugarcane field with fractures, his miraculous survival captured in a viral video. The incident highlighted the storm's ferocity and the man's narrow escape.

title: 28 People Held Hostage After 3 Killings In Manipur Released, 7 Still Captive
content: According to one of the hostages' accounts, they were blindfolded and repeatedly taken through mountainous and forested areas, moved from one location to another under armed escort.

title: Lacking Treatment Funds, Pune Man Plants 'Bomb' At Hospital To Extort Money
content: While his plan led to a 1,000-personnel manhunt, an online payment for a roll of adhesive tape became the reason behind Shivaji Rathod's arrest.

title: "No Military Solution, Negotiate Seriously": Iran's Message To US From Delhi
content: Addressing a press conference in New Delhi following a meeting of the BRICS foreign ministers, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi claimed that after the US became hopeless about achieving any goal in their war against Iran, they offered to negotiate.

title: Opinion: Blog | Yahya Khan To Munir, The Curious Pak Factor In Every US-China Thaw
content: History suggests that whenever Washington looks toward Beijing during moments of global upheaval, Islamabad finds a way back into the game.

title: India's Forex Reserves Rise By $6.3 Billion To Nearly 697 Billion
content: For the week ended May 8, foreign currency assets, a major component of the reserves, increased by USD 562 million to USD 552.387 billion, the central bank's data showed.

title: Supreme Court To Go Virtual, Judges To Car-Pool Amid Save Fuel Push
content: The move comes in the wake of a recent Office Memorandum issued by the Centre's Department of Personnel and Training on May 12, aimed at promoting fuel conservation and efficient functioning.

title: UP Woman Gives Birth To 4 Babies Over 5 Days In Rare Normal Delivery
content: Doctors delivered all four babies -- two boys and two girls -- without a Caesarean section, despite the pregnancy being categorised as 'high-risk'.

title: Ballot Papers To Be Used In Punjab Local Polls, BJP Demands EVMs
content: Punjab BJP President Sunil Jakhar-led delegation met with the State Election Commissioner and demanded that the elections be conducted using EVMs.

title: BRICS Demands Gaza Truce, Slams Tariffs At New Delhi Foreign Ministers' Meet
content: The meeting, hosted by India as the 2026 BRICS chair, brought together the foreign ministers of all ten member states.

title: Video: Endangered Primate Uses Bridge Over Rail Track In Assam Sanctuary
content: The Northeast Frontier Railway set up canopy bridges over railway tracks in Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary to facilitate the safe movement of the gibbons.

title: What Is Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool, India's Big Bet On Self-Reliance
content: India currently owns just over 1,500 ships, barely 1.2 per cent of the global shipping fleet, despite being one of the world's largest economies.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran's Araghchi Says "No Military Solution To Anything Related To Iran"
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

title: Iran War Impact: Airlines Get Relief As Maharashtra Slashes VAT On Aviation Fuel
content: Naidu thanked the Maharashtra government and Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis for acting swiftly to cut VAT on ATF.

title: "Very Slow": Supreme Court Orders Fast Track Trial In Renukaswamy Murder Case
content: The top court also perused a report from the Civil and Sessions Judge, Bengaluru, which revealed that the charge was framed in the case on November 3, 2025.

title: Bengal Suspends 3 IPS Officers Over "Lapses" In RG Kar Rape-Murder Case
content: Suvendu Adhikari clarified that the state government was not getting into the actual investigation of the crime, which is being conducted by the CBI and argued in court.

title: Opinion: Blog | The Inescapable Curse Of Being A Woman Politician In India
content: Women politicians in India are rarely allowed to exist as just politicians. They become emotional symbols. Everything except ordinary political leaders doing ordinary political things.

title: Indian dhow attacked in Omani waters, crew rescued
content: two more ships, carrying LPG, crossed the strife-hit Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday evening and Thursday morning . govt deplored the fact that commercial shipping and civilian mariners continue to be targeted . the attack led to a fire and subsequent sinking of the vessa .

title: This newly developed technology is turning carbon dioxide into 110 pounds of daily fuel
content: PC: Google Gemini The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world.
